Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
  
  Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
  
  Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
  
  ## Scope
  
  The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
  
  **Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
  
  **Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
  
  **Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
  
  Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
  
  ## Settled decisions
  
  **Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
  
  **Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
  
  | | Retriever | Director |
  |---|---|---|
  | Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
  | Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
  | Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
  
  Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
  
  **Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
  
  **Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
  
  **Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
  
  **Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
  
  **Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
  
  **RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
  
  ## Implementation conventions
  
  **Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
  
  **Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
  
  **Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
  
  **Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
  
  **Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
  
  **Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
  - **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit.
  - No pre-staged step enumeration.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
  - Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
  - Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
  - Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
  - Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
  - Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
  - Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
  
  Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
  
  Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
  
  ## Scope
  
  The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
  
  **Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
  
  **Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
  
  **Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
  
  Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
  
  ## Settled decisions
  
  **Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
  
  **Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
  
  | | Retriever | Director |
  |---|---|---|
  | Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
  | Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
  | Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
  
  Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
  
  **Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
  
  **Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
  
  **Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
  
  **Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
  
  **Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
  
  **RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
  
  ## Implementation conventions
  
  **Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
  
  **Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
  
  **Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
  
  **Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
  
  **Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
  
  **Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
  - **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit.
  - No pre-staged step enumeration.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
  - Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
  - Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
  - Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
  - Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
  - Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
  - Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

Lines 73-78 derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR + role names (RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE is exactly what the Payor probe's OAuth flow needs); lines 84-91 compose depot identity from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER but produce harmless nonsense ('d-', '-gar', '-pool', 'b-') when those are empty, and the Payor probe never reads them. 1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
92	
93	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
94	
95	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
96	
97	## References
98	
99	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
100	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
101	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
102	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
103	
104	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAN
105	
106	## Character
107	
108	Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
109	
110	Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
111	
112	Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
113	
114	## Scope
115	
116	The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
117	
118	**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
119	
120	**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
121	
122	**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
123	
124	Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
125	
126	## Settled decisions
127	
128	**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
129	
130	**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
131	
132	| | Retriever | Director |
133	|---|---|---|
134	| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
135	| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
136	| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
137	
138	Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
139	
140	**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
141	
142	**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
143	
144	**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
145	
146	**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
147	
148	**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
149	
150	**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
151	
152	## Implementation conventions
153	
154	**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
155	
156	**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
157	
158	**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
159	
160	**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
161	
162	**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
163	
164	**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
165	
166	## Cross-pace dependencies
167	
168	- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
169	- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
170	- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
171	- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
172	
173	## Constraints
174	
175	- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
176	- Real-time review at each step before commit.
177	- No pre-staged step enumeration.
178	
179	## Out of scope
180	
181	- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
182	- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
183	- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
184	- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
185	- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
186	- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
187	- Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAN

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
- Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

## Paces

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).

Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace

## Paces

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).

## Two-stage commit strategy (git-mv ancestry)

Each target file lands in two commits to keep git's rename detection
robust against wholesale content replacement:

**Stage 1 — pure rename, content unchanged:**
```
git mv RBSDK-director_knight.adoc RBSDK-director_invest.adoc
git mv RBSRK-retriever_knight.adoc RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc
git mv RBSSF-sa_forfeit.adoc RBSDD-director_divest.adoc
git mv RBSSL-sa_list.adoc RBSDR-director_roster.adoc
```
Single commit captures all four renames. `git log --follow` will trace
each new path back through the rename to the orphan's full history.

**Stage 2 — content rewrite per-file:**
Each renamed file gets its content replaced to match the new
behavioral contract (see "Behavioral-contract deltas" below). Per-file
commits keep diffs reviewable. Content replacement is wholesale — old
knight/charter/forfeit/list semantics fully gone — but the file path
through which history is traced remains intact.

**Stage 3 — author fresh siblings:**
RBSRD-retriever_divest.adoc and RBSRL-retriever_roster.adoc are written
new. Content shape mirrors the director-side sibling (which got the
ancestry), with role-specific adjustments. These two have no git
ancestry by necessity — one source can't ancestry-bear two targets.
